title: Pre-event venue rehearsal: walk-test wifi + real-phone uploads
description: Set up the full stack at the actual venue. Walk test from courtyard to dance floor on iOS and Android. End-to-end: scan QR → join wifi → land on upload page → upload 10 photos including HEIC → see them in gallery and on slideshow. Identify dead spots. Time the cold-start.

title: Decision: captive portal = opennds (nodogsplash dropped from Ubuntu 24.04)
description: nodogsplash is not in Ubuntu 24.04 apt repos (not even universe). Its maintained successor opennds (10.2.0) is. PRD stack line said 'nodogsplash (or equivalent)'; opennds is the equivalent. Decided + actioned 2026-05-16: opennds installed (masked until kgu.5). PRD §9.4/§9.6 and DEV_HANDOFF §5.2 updated. Recorded so kgu.5/kgu.6 build against opennds, whose config syntax differs from nodogsplash.
close_reason: Decided and fully actioned 2026-05-16: opennds installed (masked), PRD §9.4/§9.6 + DEV_HANDOFF §5.2 updated, kgu.6 noted. No further work — kgu.5/kgu.6 carry the implementation.

title: Day-of runbook + printed cards + MC announcement
description: Print QR cards for tables and marquee entrance. One-page runbook for the on-the-day operator (power-on order, where to look if it's broken, how to do a clean shutdown). Brief script for the MC: 'scan the code, share your photos'.

title: Slideshow page (/slideshow): auto-advance, full screen
description: Hidden /slideshow URL designed for a TV/projector browser. Auto-refreshes the most-recent N photos and crossfades between them at full screen. No-cursor CSS. Survives long uptime (no memory leak from images accumulating).

title: USB-stick export of originals + manifest
description: From the admin page: pick a mounted USB stick, copy all originals + a CSV/JSON manifest (filename, hash, uploader name, taken_at). Resume-safe.

title: Admin page: password auth, stats, hide/delete, shutdown
description: Hidden /admin URL, single device-local password (env-config). Shows storage usage, photo count, recent uploads, a button per photo to hide or delete, and a clean-shutdown button. No public link to it from the guest UI.
notes:
Built + verified 2026-05-20. Stdlib only; no new deps.
- config PHOTO_SERVER_ADMIN_PASSWORD: empty -> admin surface FAIL-CLOSED (every /admin route 404s, so a misconfigured deploy isn't open). Set -> HTTP Basic with crypto/subtle.ConstantTimeCompare on the password (PRD N11 LAN, but password protects against operator-LAN accidents).
- store: AdminPhotoRow + AdminPhotos(limit) returns visible+hidden newest-first; PhotoCounts() COALESCE-safe on empty; SetHidden(hash,bool) toggles hidden_at; DeletePhoto(hash) returns mime so the handler can clean blobs.
- server: requireAdmin gate; GET /admin dashboard (counts + storage used + fs free/total via syscall.Statfs + last 50 photos as adm-row with hide/unhide/delete forms, hidden-badge); POST /admin/photos/{hash}/{hide,unhide,delete} -> 303 to /admin (PRG, no-JS friendly); POST /admin/shutdown -> 200 'Shutting down...' page then self-SIGTERM via shutdownSignal var (test-overridable so unit runs can't kill the runner). Real SIGTERM rides the EXISTING NotifyContext path -> http.Shutdown drains + WAL checkpoints (identical to systemctl stop).
- DELETE = DB row first, then best-effort blob removal (Original/Thumb/Gallery via blobstore.Remove); filesystem+DB can't be transactional, orphans harmless.
- admin.html self-contained (no guest base layout / no bottom nav), reuses app.css + an /* admin */ block; never linked from guest UI. Confirm + danger-color delete; JS confirm() with no-JS fallback (form submits directly).
NOTE: /thumb deliberately serves hidden photos' thumbs too — admin needs to SEE hidden tiles to manage them; hash isn't guessable on N11 LAN. /photo /original /p remain visible-only (kgu.18 PhotoMeta).
QA: gofmt/vet clean, go test ./... pass (config; store: AdminPhotos/PhotoCounts COALESCE/SetHidden/DeletePhoto idempotent; admin: fail-closed no-pw, 401/WWW-Authenticate, wrong creds 401, right 200, dashboard counts+photo, hide -> feed excludes + counts move, unhide restores, delete row+blob+/photo+/original 404, bad-hash 400, shutdown-without-creds 401, shutdown signal fires via stub). Live smoke: identical behaviour end-to-end + REAL shutdown exited the process in ~400ms via the graceful drain path. Unblocks kgu.20 + kgu.21.
close_reason: Admin surface complete: hidden /admin (fail-closed if no password) + Basic auth, dashboard with counts/storage/50 recent photos with hide/unhide/delete + clean-shutdown via self-SIGTERM (existing graceful path), PRG no-JS friendly. All tests + live smoke pass incl. real process shutdown. Unblocks kgu.20 (USB export) + kgu.21 (QR PDF).

title: Full-size photo view + original download
description: Tap thumbnail → full-size view (gallery JPEG, not original) with a 'Download original' button. Swipe / arrow-key navigation between photos. Show uploader display name if set.
notes:
Built + verified 2026-05-19. Stdlib only; no new deps.
- store.PhotoMeta(hash) -> {mime,original_filename,display_name}, VISIBLE-only (hidden_at IS NULL) so direct /photo,/original,/p URLs can't bypass moderation (forward-compat with kgu.19).
- GET /photo/{hash}: browser-viewable full-size. HEIC/HEIF -> gallery JPEG (kgu.12), lazily regenerated on miss via convr (self-heal, like thumb route); JPEG/PNG -> the original (already viewable). immutable cache + ETag, http.ServeContent (Range/304).
- GET /original/{hash}: untouched original as attachment; Content-Disposition filename = sanitised original_filename else <hash><ext>. Verified byte-identical to source HEIC.
- GET /p/{hash}: server-rendered single-photo page (NO-JS fallback) via photo.html — big /photo image, 'Shared by <name>'/'anonymously', Download-original button, back-to-gallery; bottom nav active=gallery.
- viewer.js: progressive-enhancement lightbox on the gallery (tiles are real <a href=/p/hash>): intercepts clicks, overlay with arrow-key + touch-swipe nav over currently-loaded gallery tiles, uploader name from data-name, download link, Esc/backdrop/Back(pushState) close. Without JS the <a> just opens /p.
- Tiles made links across gallery (server+JS-appended), upload recent (server+JS), polaroid 'developed' shots; gallery tiles carry data-name. app.css: .cell a, .photoview, .lb lightbox (safe-area).
Scope/limit: lightbox nav spans currently-loaded gallery tiles (gallery infinite-scrolls so more load as you browse) — acceptable MVP; fetch-at-ends could be a later polish (not required by acceptance). Real touch-swipe is device-tested at kgu.25.
QA: gofmt/vet clean, go test ./... pass (PhotoMeta visible-only; jpeg view/original/fallback-name/page/hidden-404/bad-400; gallery tiles links; viewer.js; real-converter HEIC -> gallery JPEG regenerated+persisted + original attachment), make build. Live smoke: HEIC->gallery jpeg (lazy, on disk, immutable), jpeg original served, original download byte-identical, /p no-JS page, 'Shared by Aunt Sue' via JSON /session, hidden->404, bad->400, clean shutdown. Unblocks kgu.23.
close_reason: Full-size view + original download complete: /photo (HEIC->gallery JPEG lazy-regen, jpeg->original), /original (attachment, byte-identical), /p server page (no-JS), viewer.js lightbox (arrow/swipe nav, uploader name, download), tiles now links everywhere, hidden photos 404. All tests + live smoke pass. Unblocks kgu.23.

title: Gallery page: reverse-chrono grid + lazy thumbs
description: Reverse-chronological infinite-scroll grid, IntersectionObserver-based lazy load, no filters/sorts in v1. Skeleton placeholders while loading.
notes: Built + verified 2026-05-18. Stdlib only; no new deps. store.GalleryPhotos landed in the kgu.16 commit (shared infra). - server: GET /gallery renders the first 30 photos SERVER-SIDE (real , works JS-disabled per PRD §5a) + a #gal-sentinel carrying data-next-before keyset cursor. GET /api/photos?before=&limit= -> {photos,next_before} keyset pagination, visible-only (hidden_at IS NULL) newest-first via idx_photos_visible_recent. GET /static/gallery.js. - gallery.js: ES5, IntersectionObserver on the sentinel -> fetch next page -> append tiles; SECOND IO lazy-reveals each tile's image from data-src with .skeleton placeholder pulse; stops when next_before=0; degrades gracefully (no IO / no cursor -> server first page still shows). - app.css: shared .grid/.cell/.skeleton/.sentinel (added in kgu.16, reused). Scope boundary: tap-thumbnail -> full-size viewer + original download is kgu.18 — tiles are plain (no link) here; kgu.18 wraps them. No filters/sorts (v1). QA: gofmt/vet clean, go test ./... pass (empty state, 35-seed server-render=30 + non-zero cursor + newest-first, hidden excluded, /api/photos 2-page pagination, gallery.js served). Live smoke: 3 real distinct uploads -> newest-first feed, /gallery renders 3 no-JS, hide -> excluded from feed+html, limit/before pagination to end, clean shutdown.
close_reason: Gallery complete: reverse-chrono grid, server-rendered first page (works JS-disabled), IntersectionObserver infinite-scroll over /api/photos keyset pagination + IO lazy-load with skeletons, hidden photos excluded, no filters/sorts (v1). Tap-to-fullsize deferred to kgu.18 (scope boundary). All tests + live smoke pass.

title: Upload page: picker, per-file progress, resume on flake
description: Big 'Add photos' button → OS picker (multi-select). Per-file progress, retry on transient error, queue persists in localStorage so a backgrounded tab resumes. Display-name input at top, sticky. List of guest's own recent uploads underneath.
notes:
Built + verified 2026-05-18. Stdlib only; no new deps.
- store: PhotoListItem + scanPhotoList; SessionPhotos(sessionID,limit) (this issue) and GalleryPhotos(beforeID,limit) keyset (shared infra, used by kgu.17 next) — both visible-only newest-first via idx_photos_visible_recent.
- server: GET /upload renders the session's recent uploads SERVER-SIDE (works JS-disabled) + <noscript> form fallback; GET /api/uploads/mine (session-scoped JSON, isolated per session); GET /static/upload.js.
- upload.html: big 'Add photos' label wrapping <input type=file accept=image/* multiple> (NO capture -> library picker, not camera). upload.js: in-memory queue, MAX_CONCURRENT=3, per-file XHR upload.onprogress bars, retry w/ exp backoff (cap 15s) + tap-to-retry, prepends thumb to recent on success.
- Display-name 'sticky at top' = the shared sticky .appbar field; name-field wiring CENTRALISED into session.js (wireName) and removed from polaroid.js (dedupe across pages). base.html prefills name server-side via session.Ensure.
HONEST LIMIT: browsers cannot persist File bytes across reload; localStorage holds the uploaded-signature set (skip re-pick) and the server dedups by content hash, so retries are idempotent and the in-memory queue survives backgrounding. True cross-reload byte resume is impossible without re-selection — recorded; rehearsal-test at kgu.25.
QA: gofmt/vet clean, go test ./... pass (SessionPhotos/GalleryPhotos store tests + upload page/API tests), make build. Live smoke: picker no-capture, /api/uploads/mine empty->2 newest-first, server-side recent render, per-session isolation, clean shutdown. Unblocks nothing new (kgu.16 was a leaf).
close_reason: Upload page complete: multi-select photostream picker (no camera), per-file XHR progress bars, retry+backoff, localStorage uploaded-set, sticky shared name field, server-rendered + live 'your recent uploads' grid + session-scoped API. Browser File-resume limitation documented. All tests + live smoke pass.

title: Mobile-first base layout + 3-mode nav + bundled CSS (+ Polaroid)
description: Server-rendered HTML, no SPA framework. Bottom tab bar (Upload / Gallery), big tap targets, system fonts (no CDN), bundled CSS, viewport meta, dark-on-light. Works with JS disabled for the gallery; upload page needs a sprinkle of JS.
notes:
Built + verified 2026-05-18. Owner expanded 2->3 modes (Polaroid default / Upload / Gallery). Stdlib only (html/template, embed); no new deps; no build step (PRD §5a).
- internal/server/pages.go: html/template parsed from go:embed assets/templates/* (base + per-page block override); routes GET /{$} (exact root, not catch-all -> unknown still 404), GET /upload, GET /gallery, GET /static/app.css, GET /static/polaroid.js. Reuses session.go embed/serve pattern.
- base.html: viewport-fit=cover safe-area, system fonts (no CDN N1), dark-on-light, fixed 3-tab bottom nav (<a> links so Gallery works no-JS), optional shared name field -> POST /session (kgu.14), session.js included.
- Polaroid (functional): <input type=file accept=image/* capture=environment> -> native camera -> polaroid.js auto POST /upload (credentials same-origin, session cookie kgu.14), instant objectURL 'developing' tile -> swaps to uploaded[0].thumb_url (kgu.13), input reset for next shot, tap-to-retry on failure. getUserMedia live viewfinder NOT possible on plain-HTTP LAN (PRD §9.7) -> decided <input capture> (owner-confirmed). Upload/Gallery = navigable placeholders (kgu.16/kgu.17 own them).
- PRD §5a updated (2->3 modes, Polaroid default, camera/HTTP constraint). Follow-up bead filed for rehearsal-grade hardening; native capture device-tested at kgu.25.
QA: gofmt/vet clean, go test ./... pass (pages_test: 3-tab nav, /=Polaroid+capture, no external URLs N1, gallery no-JS, 404 preserved), make build. Live smoke: /,/upload,/gallery 200; assets correct CT; unknown 404; Polaroid path (GET /session->POST /upload) stores+session-tags photo; clean shutdown. Unblocks kgu.16, kgu.17.
close_reason: 3-mode UI shell complete: server-rendered html/template base + bundled CSS + fixed bottom nav (Polaroid default / Upload / Gallery), functional Polaroid (native-camera capture -> auto-upload, reuses /upload + session + thumb), offline-first (no CDN), Gallery works JS-disabled. PRD §5a reconciled. All tests + live smoke pass. Unblocks kgu.16/kgu.17.

title: Persistent guest session (cookie + localStorage)
description: Issue a long-lived random token in an httpOnly cookie + a copy in localStorage on first page load. Display name stored against the session. No expiry within the event window. Resilient to iOS Private Relay / Safari quirks.
notes:
Built + verified 2026-05-18. Stdlib only (crypto/rand, encoding/base64, embed); no new deps.
- internal/session.Manager: 256-bit base64url token; Ensure (cookie or mint), Adopt (rebind a client-presented localStorage token after cookie loss; junk -> fresh), SetDisplayName, ValidToken. Cookie: HttpOnly, Secure=FALSE (plain-HTTP LAN PRD F4/§9.7), SameSite=Lax, Path=/, Max-Age=SessionMaxAge (default 30d, PRD 'no expiry in event window'), refreshed every visit.
- server: GET/POST /session (Cache-Control no-store) returns {token,display_name} so JS can mirror the HttpOnly token to localStorage; POST {token} = cookie-loss recovery (Safari/Private Relay resilience), POST {display_name} sets name. GET /static/session.js = embedded ES5 bundle (offline N1) that GETs on first contact / POSTs saved token on return, mirrors to localStorage, fires ps:session event.
- upload.go refactored to use session.Manager.Ensure (kgu.11 seam closed; removed ad-hoc requestSessionID + per-part UpsertSession); display_name form field persists to the session.
- config SESSION_MAX_AGE. server.Deps.Sessions.
Security: PRD N11 trusted LAN — token is opaque continuity key, not adversarial auth; losing it only re-prompts name (R6).
QA: gofmt/vet clean, go test ./... pass (new session pkg + server session/upload tests), make build. Live smoke: establish cookie (HttpOnly,no Secure,Lax,30d) + token JSON, set name, cookie-loss POST rebinds same identity+name, session.js served, upload tags photo+session. Unblocks kgu.16.
close_reason: Persistent guest session complete: 256-bit token in HttpOnly cookie (Secure=false for HTTP LAN, 30d), localStorage mirror via bundled /static/session.js, cookie-loss recovery (Private Relay/Safari) by re-presenting the saved token, display name stored against session. Closed the kgu.11 upload seam. All tests + live smoke pass. Unblocks kgu.16.

title: Thumbnail generation pipeline
description: Generate ~400px webp thumbnails on upload. Lazy-regenerate on miss. Cache-Control: immutable since thumbs are content-addressed.
notes:
Built + verified 2026-05-17. Reuses kgu.12 convert pipeline; stdlib only.
- convert refactor: generic render() shared by GalleryJPEG (.jpg) + Thumbnail (.webp); NewConverter(galleryPx,galleryQ,thumbPx,thumbQ). Pool worker now: Thumbnail for EVERY photo + GalleryJPEG only for HEIC/HEIF (Job.MIME). ExtForMIME helper.
- GET /thumb/{hash}: serves thumbs/<hash>.webp. Cache-Control public,max-age=31536000,immutable + ETag=hash (content-addressed); http.ServeContent gives If-None-Match->304. Lazy-regenerate on miss (sync Converter.Thumbnail via store.PhotoByHash->ExtForMIME, 20s ctx); bad hash->400, unknown->404.
- store: HEICPhotos replaced with AllPhotos (thumbs needed for all); PhotoByHash. main backfillRenditions: enqueue if thumb missing OR (heic & gallery missing) - self-heal N8. config THUMB_PX(400)/THUMB_QUALITY(80). server.Deps gains Conv (sync converter for lazy path).
- upload enqueues every photo (not just heic) off hot path.
QA: gofmt/vet clean, go test ./... pass (convert+server run REAL vipsthumbnail), make build. Live smoke: heic+jpeg both get 300x400 webp thumbs, gallery jpeg heic-only, cache headers+304, lazy-regen after delete, backfill regenerated 2 after restart, SIGTERM clean. Unblocks kgu.17 (gallery grid).
close_reason: Thumbnail pipeline complete: ~400px webp for every photo via the shared vipsthumbnail pool (off hot path), GET /thumb/{hash} with immutable content-addressed caching + ETag/304, lazy-regenerate-on-miss, startup backfill self-heal. All tests (real libvips) + live smoke pass. Unblocks kgu.17.

title: HEIC → JPEG conversion via libvips
description: Use libvips (govips or shelling out to vipsthumbnail) to produce a browser-viewable JPEG for HEIC originals. Original always preserved. Convert off the upload hot path if it slows the response.
notes:
Built + verified 2026-05-17. Shell out to vipsthumbnail (user-approved; no cgo, single static binary preserved, libvips fault isolated to child - N8). Stdlib only (os/exec), no new Go deps.
- internal/convert/convert.go: GalleryJPEG runs 'vipsthumbnail <orig> --size MAXxMAX -o tmp[Q,strip]' -> blobstore.Put(Gallery) (atomic+fsync). Downscale-only, EXIF auto-rotate, metadata stripped (privacy N2). Idempotent (skip if exists). Original only read, never modified (verified byte-identical).
- internal/convert/pool.go: bounded worker pool (default min(NumCPU,4), PRD R5), non-blocking de-duped Enqueue (queue-full -> defer to backfill), ctx-cancel shutdown + Stop() drain.
- upload.go enqueues HEIC/HEIF off the hot path (PRD R4/R5); response returns immediately, JPEG ~1.4s later.
- main: startup backfill re-enqueues HEIC photos missing a gallery jpeg (crash self-heal, N8). store.HEICPhotos(). config knobs CONVERT_WORKERS/GALLERY_MAX_PX/JPEG_QUALITY/VIPSTHUMBNAIL_BIN. server.New refactored to Deps struct.
Scope: HEIC/HEIF only (jpeg/png gallery-jpeg deferred to kgu.17/18, like the kgu.11/14 split).
QA: gofmt/vet clean, go test ./... pass (convert tests run REAL vipsthumbnail on classic-car.heic), make build. Live smoke: async convert, original preserved byte-identical, 3024x4032->1200x1600 capped+rotated, backfill regenerated after restart, SIGTERM clean.
close_reason: HEIC->JPEG via vipsthumbnail shell-out complete: async bounded worker pool off the upload hot path, original preserved untouched, EXIF auto-rotate + metadata strip, idempotent, startup backfill self-heal. No cgo. All tests (real libvips) + live smoke pass.

title: Upload endpoint: multipart, dedup, EXIF read
description: POST /upload, multi-file. Read EXIF DateTimeOriginal for taken_at. Dedup by content hash. Tag with uploader session. Returns thumbnail URLs for the just-uploaded set.
notes:
Built + verified 2026-05-17. Stdlib only (no new deps).
- internal/exif: hand-rolled DateTimeOriginal. JPEG APP1 walk + HEIF 'Exif\0\0'-signature scan (first 1MiB; iterates occurrences, skips the infe item-type decl, uses the one followed by a real TIFF header) -> TIFF IFD0->ExifIFD->tag 0x9003. recover() guard; NULL on anything malformed. Verified against REAL iPhone classic-car.heic -> 2023-10-22 09:39:48.
- internal/store: UpsertSession (ON CONFLICT keep-name-if-empty), InsertPhoto (ON CONFLICT(content_hash) DO NOTHING -> returns existing id + deduped=true), PhotoTakenAt. FK-safe.
- internal/server: POST /upload streams parts (no full buffering, N4); sniff+ext detect (jpeg/png/heic/heif, else per-file 415, siblings continue); PutOriginal (atomic/dedup) -> EXIF from stored blob -> InsertPhoto; session from ps_session cookie or session_id/display_name; per-file JSON {hash,mime,deduped,photo_id,thumb_url,original_url}; limitedReader caps MaxUploadBytes.
- config: PHOTO_SERVER_MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES (default 64MiB). main wires st/blobs into server.New.
Boundary: kgu.11 consumes a session id; secure cookie/localStorage issuance stays kgu.14.
QA: gofmt/vet clean, go test ./... all pass, make build, live smoke (real HEIC: image/heic + taken_at correct + on-disk shard; re-upload dedups to 1 row; session upserted; .txt->415; healthz+SIGTERM clean). Unblocks kgu.12, kgu.16.
close_reason: Upload endpoint complete: streaming multipart POST /upload, sha256 dedup, hand-rolled EXIF DateTimeOriginal (verified on real iPhone HEIC), session tagging (FK-safe), per-file JSON with thumb/original URLs, size cap. Stdlib-only. All tests + live smoke pass. Unblocks kgu.12 (HEIC->JPEG) and kgu.16 (upload UI).

title: On-disk layout for originals + thumbnails (content-hash dedup)
description: originals/<hash[:2]>/<hash>.<ext>, thumbs/<hash[:2]>/<hash>.webp, gallery_jpegs/<hash[:2]>/<hash>.jpg (HEIC fallback). Atomic write (tmp + rename) with fsync.
notes:
Built + verified 2026-05-17. Stdlib only (crypto/sha256, os, io), no new deps.
- internal/blobstore: Kind {originals,thumbs,gallery_jpegs}; key = SHA-256 of ORIGINAL content; sharded <kind>/<hash[:2]>/<hash><ext> (thumb=.webp, gallery=.jpg fixed). PutOriginal streams+hashes to temp then places; Put(kind,hash,ext) for derived renditions filed under original's hash; Path/Open/Exists/Remove.
- Atomic+durable: CreateTemp on same fs -> fsync file -> MkdirAll shard -> rename -> fsync shard dir (PRD N6: ack'd upload survives power-off). Idempotent dedup (skip rewrite if present). validHash rejects non-sha256.
- main.go: blobstore.New(DataDir) at startup -> creates kind dirs (first-boot ready).
QA: gofmt/vet clean, go test ./... pass (hash/shard/dedup/idempotent/derived/badhash/remove/no-tmp-leftover), make build OK, live smoke (originals|thumbs|gallery_jpegs created, healthz, SIGTERM clean). Unblocks kgu.11/13/20/24.
close_reason: Content-addressed on-disk layout complete: sharded originals/thumbs/gallery_jpegs keyed by sha256, atomic tmp+rename with file+dir fsync (N6 durable), idempotent dedup. Stdlib-only. All tests + live smoke pass. Unblocks kgu.11 (upload), kgu.13 (thumbs), kgu.20 (USB export), kgu.24 (rsync).

title: SQLite schema + migrations (photos, sessions)
description: Tables: photos(id, content_hash UNIQUE, mime, original_filename, uploader_session_id, display_name, taken_at, uploaded_at, width, height, hidden_at), sessions(id, display_name, created_at, last_seen_at). Schema-versioned migrations applied at startup.
notes:
Built + verified 2026-05-16. Driver: modernc.org/sqlite v1.50.1 (pure Go, no cgo - keeps single static binary; first 3rd-party dep, user-approved). Migrations: hand-rolled go:embed + PRAGMA user_version (user-approved).
- internal/store/store.go: Open() with DSN _pragma per-conn: journal_mode=WAL (concurrent gallery reads, N4), synchronous=FULL (ack'd upload survives power-off, N6), foreign_keys=ON, busy_timeout=5000.
- internal/store/migrate.go: embedded NNNN_name.sql, applied in tx, idempotent; rejects malformed/dup versions.
- migrations/0001_init.sql: sessions(id,display_name,created_at,last_seen_at); photos(11 cols per spec, content_hash UNIQUE, uploader_session_id FK->sessions, hidden_at nullable); partial idx idx_photos_visible_recent (uploaded_at DESC WHERE hidden_at IS NULL) for gallery F8; idx_photos_session for kgu.16/admin. Time cols = INTEGER unix secs UTC.
- cmd/photo-server: opens DB at <DataDir>/photo-server.db, migrates at startup, logs schema_version, closes on shutdown.
QA: gofmt/vet clean, go test ./... pass (migrations idempotent, pragmas, UNIQUE, FK enforcement), make build OK, live smoke (schema_version=1, tables/cols/indexes via sqlite3, healthz, SIGTERM clean). Unblocks kgu.10/14/19.
close_reason: SQLite schema + versioned migrations complete: photos+sessions per spec, content_hash UNIQUE, FK enforced, WAL+synchronous=FULL durability, hand-rolled go:embed migrations applied idempotently at startup. All tests + live smoke pass. Unblocks kgu.10 (on-disk layout), kgu.14 (sessions), kgu.19 (admin).

title: Go service skeleton + systemd unit
description: go module, single binary, config via env or a small toml/yaml, structured logging to file + journald. systemd unit with Restart=on-failure, ProtectSystem, working dir for SQLite/photos. Health-check endpoint.
notes:
Built + verified 2026-05-16. Stdlib-only (offline-first, zero third-party deps, no go.sum). Module github.com/turbomerl/photo-server, Go 1.26.3.
- cmd/photo-server: config load, slog logger, signal.NotifyContext SIGINT/SIGTERM graceful shutdown.
- internal/config: env-only (PHOTO_SERVER_ADDR/DATA_DIR/LOG_LEVEL/LOG_FILE/SHUTDOWN_TIMEOUT), honours systemd $STATE_DIRECTORY. Tested.
- internal/server: net/http (1.22 mux), GET /healthz -> {status:ok,version}, debug access log, bounded ReadHeaderTimeout, graceful Shutdown. Tested (healthz/404/405/shutdown).
- deploy/photo-server.service: User=photo-server, Restart=on-failure, StateDirectory=photo-server, ProtectSystem=strict + hardening, AmbientCapabilities=CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE. MemoryDenyWriteExecute deliberately OFF (re-eval at kgu.12/libvips cgo).
- Makefile + CLAUDE.md Build&Test filled in.
QA: gofmt clean, go vet OK, go test ./... pass, make build OK, live smoke (healthz 200, 404, data dir 0750, SIGTERM clean exit 0), systemd-analyze verify clean. Unblocks kgu.9 + kgu.15.
close_reason: Service skeleton complete: stdlib-only Go binary, env config, slog to journald+file, /healthz, hardened systemd unit. All acceptance items met; gofmt/vet/test/build/live-smoke/systemd-verify all pass. Unblocks kgu.9 (SQLite) and kgu.15 (UI base).

title: Captive-portal redirect to upload page
description: Use nodogsplash or equivalent to trigger iOS/Android captive-portal popup on join, landing on the upload URL. Accept ~5% of guests will need to open the URL manually; print URL on QR card too.
notes: Captive portal engine = opennds (NOT nodogsplash — gone from Ubuntu 24.04). opennds 10.2.0 installed but masked; this ticket owns unmasking + config. Config syntax/paths differ from nodogsplash docs — use opennds docs.

title: Configure Dell as wifi gateway: hostapd + dnsmasq + routing
description: Set up the Dell to bridge: ethernet to AP serves a private LAN with DHCP (dnsmasq), DNS hijack to local, no upstream NAT (no internet). WPA2-PSK on the AP, single SSID. Document the netplan/systemd-networkd config in repo.

title: Mount marquee AP near courtyard-facing entrance
description: High mount, line-of-sight to dance floor, near the courtyard-facing entrance so coverage bleeds outward. PoE-fed from the switch over the Cat6 run.

title: Run outdoor Cat6 from house to marquee alongside mains feed
description: Piggyback the marquee mains-feed trench/route. Use direct-burial / UV-resistant cable. Terminate cleanly both ends. Test with a cable tester before the event.
notes:
Decisions (2026-05-13):
- Injector lives at the HOUSE end (near the Dell, indoors, dry, mains-powered).
- Only the outdoor Cat6 run carries data + 24 V PoE; the marquee end needs NO mains for the network — AP is powered down the Cat6.
- Topology:
    [Dell] -short indoor patch-> [PoE injector + mains] -long outdoor Cat6-> [AP in marquee]
- Watch: passive 24 V on Cat6 starts to suffer voltage drop past ~50–60 m. If the run is longer, flag for shielded cable or a gigabit injector with more current headroom.
- Test with a cable tester both ends before mounting the AP.

title: Confirm Dell mini-PC suitability for the day
description: Acceptance: ≥150GB free disk, working gigabit ethernet, sustains HEIC→JPEG transcode at ~20 concurrent uploads in a soak test, runs Ubuntu cleanly. If any check fails, decide whether to buy storage or fall back to laptop.
notes:
Hardware verification (Dell, 2026-05-16, Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS, kernel 6.17):
- DISK: /dev/sda6 279G, 235G free on / -> PASS (acceptance: >=150GB).
- NIC: eno1 gigabit present (DOWN only = no cable) -> PASS (gigabit ethernet for AP backhaul).
- CPU/RAM: 8 cores, 15GiB RAM, 4GiB swap -> ample headroom.
- OS: Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS runs cleanly.
- Toolchain installed: libvips 8.15.1 (+libheif 1.17.6, plugins libde265/dav1d/aomenc), Go 1.26.3, pkg-config, sqlite3.
PENDING: single-file HEIC->JPEG smoke test (awaiting real phone HEIC).
NOT DONE: ~20-concurrent HEIC->JPEG soak test from acceptance criteria - rehearsal-grade, see follow-up bead.
HEIC->JPEG smoke test PASS (2026-05-16): real iPhone 12 Pro HEIC (HEVC Main Still, 3024x4032, Apple EXIF) -> vips copy exit 0 -> valid JPEG 3024x4032, EXIF preserved, dimensions match. libvips 8.15.1 + libheif 1.17.6 + libde265 decode path verified. PRD R2 mitigated (single-file). ~20-concurrent soak test deferred to photo_server-o5j (rehearsal-grade, run under kgu.25).
close_reason: Dell suitability confirmed: 235GB free (>=150), eno1 gigabit NIC, 8c/15GiB, clean Ubuntu 24.04.4, toolchain installed, real-phone HEIC->JPEG smoke test passes. ~20-concurrent soak test tracked separately as photo_server-o5j (rehearsal-grade). Dell is suitable; laptop-fallback not needed.

title: Procure hardware: UAP-AC-LR (ordered) + cables + (maybe) USB SSD
description: Buy: 1× Ubiquiti U6-Lite (PoE AP), 1× small PoE switch (or PoE injector + plain switch), outdoor-rated direct-burial Cat6 (length = house→marquee + ~5m slack), RJ45 ends/keystones if needed. Add a 256GB+ USB SSD only if Dell internal storage <150GB free.
notes:
Decisions (2026-05-13):
- AP: Ubiquiti UniFi AP AC LR — ORDERED. Wi-Fi 5, dual-band (2.4 GHz 3x3, 5 GHz 2x2), longer-range antennas suit marquee+courtyard layout. Realistic ~80–100 concurrent clients, comfortable for our ~80-phone peak.
- PoE: passive 24 V (NOT 802.3af/at). Use the bundled injector that ships in the box — no PoE switch needed.
- BOM simplifies to: AP (with bundled injector) + cables. No managed switch required for v1.

Cables to order:
- 1 × short indoor Cat6 patch (~0.5–1 m): Dell → injector LAN port.
- 1 × outdoor-rated direct-burial Cat6 (length = house → marquee + ~5 m slack, gel-filled / UV-resistant): injector POE port → AP in marquee.

Pending:
- USB SSD only if kgu.2 (Dell disk check) shows <150 GB free.

title: Wedding photo-server v1
description: Single-day, one-off LAN photo upload appliance for the owner's wedding (≤150 guests, courtyard+marquee venue, no internet). See docs/PRD.md.

title: Polaroid mode hardening (multi-shot queue, offline retry, device camera quirks)
description: kgu.15 shipped a functional minimal Polaroid (capture -> auto POST /upload, developing strip, tap-to-retry). Hardening for the day: bounded concurrent in-flight uploads / ordered queue, resilient offline retry + persistence across tab backgrounding (cf kgu.16 queue), iOS/Android native-camera return quirks (HEIC orientation already handled server-side via kgu.12), large-burst behaviour. Real native-camera capture is NOT curl-automatable — must be device-tested at the venue rehearsal (kgu.25).

title: HEIC->JPEG ~20-concurrent transcode soak test on the Dell
description: kgu.2 acceptance includes sustaining ~20 concurrent HEIC->JPEG transcodes. kgu.2 was closed on the hardware checks (disk/NIC/CPU/OS/toolchain) + single-file smoke test only. This rehearsal-grade soak test is the remaining acceptance item; run it under kgu.25 (venue rehearsal) conditions with real phone HEICs. If the Dell can't sustain it, the laptop-fallback decision (kgu.2) reopens.

title: Periodic rsync of originals to USB stick
description: systemd timer rsyncs originals/ to a labelled USB stick every N minutes during the event. Tolerant of stick being removed.

title: QR code PDF generator (wifi join + URL)
description: Admin generates a printable A6/A5 PDF with: WIFI: URI QR (auto-joins SSID/passphrase on iOS+Android), URL QR, plain-text URL. Multiple per page for table cards.

title: Friendly hostname (mDNS + DNS hijack to e.g. photos.wedding)
description: Resolve a friendly name on the LAN via dnsmasq wildcard so the URL fits on a printed card. mDNS as a fallback for desktop browsers.

title: Anonymous hearts (couple-only post-event view) — only if cheap
description: Tap a heart on a photo. Counts NEVER shown to other guests. Surfaced only on the admin page as a 'guest favourites' sort post-event. Skip entirely if it adds non-trivial complexity.
